test_cases.description: Verify that the afc binary is built with Cobra and exposes top-level subcommands login and keys.
preconditions: github.com/spf13/cobra dependency is present in go.mod
afc binary is compiled via `go build ./cmd/afc`
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout or stderr contains the subcommand names `login` and `keys`; binary name is `afc`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: output = exec('afc --help'); assert output contains 'login'; assert output contains 'keys'; assert exit_code == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that --hub-url flag overrides AF_HUB_URL env var and that either source is accepted.
preconditions: A stub hub server is listening at http://localhost:9001
test_cases.expected: afc makes the HTTP request to http://localhost:9001, not to http://env-hub.example.com; --hub-url value takes precedence
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub = start_stub_server(port=9001); set_env('AF_HUB_URL', 'http://env-hub.example.com'); exec('afc keys list --hub-url http://localhost:9001 --api-key test-key'); assert stub.received_request == true; assert stub.requested_host == 'localhost:9001'

test_cases.description: Verify that JSON output goes to stdout and human-readable messages go to stderr.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns a valid JSON array from the list-keys endpoint
test_cases.input: Run `afc keys list --api-key test-key` with stdout and stderr captured separately
test_cases.expected: stdout contains valid JSON; stderr contains no JSON; any status messages appear only on stderr
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key test-key'); assert is_valid_json(stdout); assert not is_valid_json(stderr) OR stderr is empty

test_cases.description: Verify that afc exits with a non-zero exit code and prints an error to stderr when a command fails.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 500 for all requests
test_cases.expected: Exit code is non-zero; stderr contains a human-readable error message
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key test-key'); assert exit_code != 0; assert len(stderr) > 0

test_cases.description: Verify that running afc without a subcommand or with --help prints usage info and exits 0.
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0 for both; output contains usage text with available subcommands and global flags listed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: output1, exit1 = exec_capture('afc'); output2, exit2 = exec_capture('afc --help'); assert exit1 == 0; assert exit2 == 0; assert 'login' in output1 OR 'login' in output2; assert '--hub-url' in output1 OR '--hub-url' in output2

test_cases.description: Verify that afc login fetches the provider list and validates the requested provider exists before proceeding.
preconditions: A stub hub server is configured to return a provider list containing only 'github' from GET /api/v1/auth/providers
test_cases.expected: afc issues GET /api/v1/auth/providers; github is validated; flow proceeds to open browser
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub = start_stub_server(port=9001, route='GET /api/v1/auth/providers', response={providers:['github']}); exec_async('afc login --provider github'); assert stub.received_request_for('GET /api/v1/auth/providers'); assert stub.request_count('GET /api/v1/auth/providers') == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that afc login starts a local callback server on a random available port and constructs a redirect_uri from the local address.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns provider list including github
Browser open command is mocked to capture the URL
test_cases.expected: A local HTTP server starts on a random port; the browser open command receives a URL containing redirect_uri=http://localhost:<random_port>/callback
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock_browser = capture_browser_open_calls(); exec_async('afc login --provider github'); auth_url = mock_browser.last_called_url; assert 'redirect_uri' in auth_url; redirect = parse_query_param(auth_url, 'redirect_uri'); assert redirect starts_with 'http://localhost:'; port = parse_port(redirect); assert port > 0 AND port <= 65535; assert is_port_listening(port)

test_cases.description: Verify that afc login sends the authorization code to POST /api/v1/auth/callback and prints the returned user object as JSON to stdout.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns provider list including github from GET /api/v1/auth/providers
A stub hub server returns a user object from POST /api/v1/auth/callback
Local callback server port is known
test_cases.input: Simulate OAuth redirect to the local callback server with ?code=test-code-123
test_cases.expected: afc POSTs to /api/v1/auth/callback with the authorization code; stdout contains the JSON user object; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub = start_stub_server(port=9001); user_obj = {id:'u1', email:'op@example.com'}; stub.on('POST /api/v1/auth/callback').return(user_obj); login_proc = exec_async('afc login --provider github'); callback_port = get_callback_port(login_proc); http_get('http://localhost:' + callback_port + '/callback?code=test-code-123'); stdout, _, exit_code = login_proc.wait(); assert exit_code == 0; assert is_valid_json(stdout); parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed.id == 'u1'

test_cases.description: Verify that the local callback server is shut down and its port released after the login flow completes.
preconditions: A stub hub server is configured to serve the full login flow
test_cases.input: Run `afc login --provider github` to completion (success or failure)
test_cases.expected: After the process exits, no process is listening on the previously used callback port
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: login_proc = exec_async('afc login --provider github'); callback_port = get_callback_port(login_proc); complete_login_flow(callback_port); login_proc.wait(); assert not is_port_listening(callback_port)

test_cases.description: Verify that afc keys create sends a create request authenticated with --api-key and defaults expiry to 30 days when --expires is omitted.
preconditions: A stub hub server is configured to accept POST for key creation and return a key object with plaintext secret
test_cases.input: Run `afc keys create --workspace ws-123 --label ci-bot --api-key myapikey`
test_cases.expected: afc sends an authenticated create request; request body includes workspace_id=ws-123, label=ci-bot, expires_days=30; stdout contains JSON key object with plaintext secret; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub = start_stub_server(port=9001); key_obj = {key_id:'k1', secret:'plaintext-secret', workspace_id:'ws-123'}; stub.on_create_key().return(key_obj); stdout, _, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys create --workspace ws-123 --label ci-bot --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code == 0; req = stub.last_request('POST /api/v1/keys'); assert req.auth_header == 'myapikey'; assert req.body.workspace_id == 'ws-123'; assert req.body.expires_days == 30; assert parse_json(stdout).secret == 'plaintext-secret'

test_cases.description: Verify that afc keys create prints the plaintext key secret to stdout exactly once and never to stderr.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns a key object with a known plaintext secret value 'super-secret-xyz'
test_cases.input: Run `afc keys create --workspace ws-123 --api-key myapikey` with stdout and stderr captured separately
test_cases.expected: The string 'super-secret-xyz' appears exactly once in stdout; it does not appear in stderr
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys create --workspace ws-123 --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code == 0; assert stdout.count('super-secret-xyz') == 1; assert 'super-secret-xyz' not in stderr

test_cases.description: Verify that afc keys list fetches all keys and prints a JSON array with required fields to stdout.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns a JSON array of two key objects from GET /api/v1/keys
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout is a valid JSON array; each element contains key_id, label, workspace_id, expires_at, created_at, and a revoked status field
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('GET /api/v1/keys').return([{key_id:'k1',label:'bot',workspace_id:'ws-1',expires_at:'2026-01-01',created_at:'2025-01-01',revoked:false},{key_id:'k2',label:'ci',workspace_id:'ws-2',expires_at:null,created_at:'2025-06-01',revoked:true}]); stdout, _, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code == 0; arr = parse_json(stdout); assert is_array(arr); assert len(arr) == 2; for item in arr: assert 'key_id' in item; assert 'label' in item; assert 'workspace_id' in item; assert 'expires_at' in item; assert 'created_at' in item; assert 'revoked' in item

test_cases.description: Verify that afc keys refresh sends a refresh request and prints the new key object with new plaintext secret to stdout.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns an updated key object with new_secret='refreshed-secret-abc' from the refresh endpoint
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; afc sends a refresh request for key-id-42; stdout contains valid JSON with the new plaintext secret 'refreshed-secret-abc'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('POST /api/v1/keys/key-id-42/refresh').return({key_id:'key-id-42',secret:'refreshed-secret-abc'}); stdout, _, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys refresh key-id-42 --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code == 0; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed.secret == 'refreshed-secret-abc'; assert stub.received_request_for('POST /api/v1/keys/key-id-42/refresh')

test_cases.description: Verify that afc keys revoke sends a revoke request and prints a confirmation message to stderr.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 200 from the revoke endpoint for key-id-42
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; afc sends a revoke request for key-id-42; stderr contains a non-empty confirmation message; stdout is empty or contains no key data
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('DELETE /api/v1/keys/key-id-42').return(200); stdout, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys revoke key-id-42 --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code == 0; assert len(stderr) > 0; assert 'revok' in stderr.lower() OR 'success' in stderr.lower() OR 'key-id-42' in stderr

test_cases.description: Verify that afc parses the hub error envelope and prints a human-readable message to stderr on any non-2xx response.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 403 with body {error:'forbidden', message:'You do not have access'} from GET /api/v1/keys
test_cases.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'You do not have access' or equivalent human-readable text from the error envelope; stdout is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('GET /api/v1/keys').return(403, {error:'forbidden', message:'You do not have access'}); stdout, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'You do not have access' in stderr; assert stdout is empty

test_cases.description: Verify that afc falls back to printing the raw HTTP status code and response body when the error body is not a valid error envelope.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 502 with a plain-text body 'Bad Gateway' from GET /api/v1/keys
test_cases.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains '502' and 'Bad Gateway' (raw status and body)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('GET /api/v1/keys').return(502, 'Bad Gateway', content_type='text/plain'); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert '502' in stderr; assert 'Bad Gateway' in stderr

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/cli.md exists and documents all commands, flags, environment variables, and includes at least one example per command.
preconditions: The repository has been cloned or built
docs/cli.md file exists at the repository root
test_cases.expected: File exists; contains sections for login, keys create, keys list, keys refresh, keys revoke; documents --hub-url, AF_HUB_URL, --api-key, AF_HUB_API_KEY; includes at least one usage example per command
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: content = read_file('docs/cli.md'); assert len(content) > 0; for cmd in ['login', 'keys create', 'keys list', 'keys refresh', 'keys revoke']: assert cmd in content; for item in ['--hub-url', 'AF_HUB_URL', '--api-key', 'AF_HUB_API_KEY']: assert item in content; assert content contains example code blocks for each command

test_cases.description: Verify that README.md contains a link to docs/cli.md.
preconditions: README.md exists at the repository root
test_cases.expected: README.md contains a hyperlink or reference to docs/cli.md

property_tests.description: For any outcome of afc login (success, timeout, provider error, hub error, or SIGINT), the local callback server is always shut down and its port is released before the process exits.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each outcome variant: (a) successful flow, (b) timeout after N seconds, (c) provider error redirect, (d) hub POST error, (e) SIGINT during wait — run afc login and capture the callback port
property_tests.invariant_check: After login_proc.wait() completes in each variant, assert not is_port_listening(callback_port); assert no_socket_in_TIME_WAIT_on(callback_port)

property_tests.description: For any successful execution of afc keys create or afc keys refresh, the plaintext key secret appears on stdout exactly once and never on stderr or any other stream.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any valid workspace_id, label, and expiry value passed to keys create; and for any valid key_id passed to keys refresh — stub hub returns a key object with a known plaintext secret value SECRET
property_tests.invariant_check: stdout, stderr = exec_capture(cmd); assert stdout.count(SECRET) == 1; assert SECRET not in stderr; assert exit_code == 0

property_tests.description: For any afc command invocation, machine-readable JSON output is written exclusively to stdout and all human-readable messages (errors, confirmations, status) are written exclusively to stderr.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any command in {login, keys create, keys list, keys refresh, keys revoke} with any combination of valid/invalid inputs — capture stdout and stderr separately
property_tests.invariant_check: if stdout is non-empty: assert is_valid_json(stdout); if stderr is non-empty: assert not is_pure_json_object_or_array(stderr); assert no_json_key_object appears in stderr that is not also in stdout

property_tests.description: For any command that encounters an error condition, the process always exits with a non-zero exit code and emits at least one non-empty error message to stderr — this property also verifies termination and bounded retry behavior.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any error condition in {hub unreachable, invalid provider, callback timeout, hub HTTP error, missing flags, missing credentials, missing key_id} — run the relevant afc command and capture exit code and stderr; also verify that the command terminates within a bounded time (≤ 10 seconds for non-timeout errors, ≤ configured_timeout + 2s for timeout errors)
property_tests.invariant_check: exit_code, stderr, elapsed = exec_timed(cmd, error_env); assert exit_code != 0; assert len(stderr.strip()) > 0; assert elapsed <= max_allowed_seconds(error_type); assert no_infinite_retry_loop_detected()

property_tests.description: For any command invocation where both --hub-url flag and AF_HUB_URL env var are set to different values, the --hub-url flag value always takes precedence.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any command in {keys list, keys create, keys refresh, keys revoke, login} — set AF_HUB_URL to URL_A and pass --hub-url URL_B where URL_A != URL_B; use a request-capturing stub at URL_B
property_tests.invariant_check: stub_B = start_capturing_stub(URL_B); set_env('AF_HUB_URL', URL_A); exec(cmd + ' --hub-url ' + URL_B + ' ...'); assert stub_B.received_any_request(); stub_A = get_request_log(URL_A); assert stub_A.received_no_requests()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc prints a descriptive error and exits non-zero when neither --hub-url nor AF_HUB_URL is set.
edge_case_tests.input: Run `afc keys list --api-key myapikey` with no --hub-url flag and AF_HUB_URL unset
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message describing the missing hub URL configuration
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: unset_env('AF_HUB_URL'); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'hub' in stderr.lower() OR 'url' in stderr.lower() OR 'AF_HUB_URL' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc login prints a connection error with hub URL details and exits non-zero when the hub is unreachable.
preconditions: No service is listening at http://localhost:19999
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'http://localhost:19999' and connection failure details
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc login --provider github --hub-url http://localhost:19999'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'localhost:19999' in stderr; assert any(word in stderr.lower() for word in ['connect', 'unreachable', 'refused', 'dial'])

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc login times out after 5 minutes of waiting for the OAuth callback, shuts down the callback server, and prints a retry suggestion.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns a provider list including github
The callback timeout is configured to a short value (e.g. 5 seconds) for testing via a test flag or environment variable
edge_case_tests.input: Run `afc login --provider github` and never simulate the OAuth redirect
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a timeout error and a retry suggestion; the callback port is no longer listening after exit
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: login_proc = exec_async('afc login --provider github', env={'CALLBACK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS':'5'}); callback_port = get_callback_port(login_proc); _, stderr, exit_code = login_proc.wait(timeout=15); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'timeout' in stderr.lower() OR 'timed out' in stderr.lower(); assert 'retry' in stderr.lower() OR 'again' in stderr.lower(); assert not is_port_listening(callback_port)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc login shuts down the callback server and prints the provider's error description when the OAuth provider sends an error parameter.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns a provider list including github
edge_case_tests.input: Simulate OAuth redirect to callback server with ?error=access_denied&error_description=User+denied+access
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'access_denied' or 'User denied access'; callback port is no longer listening
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: login_proc = exec_async('afc login --provider github'); callback_port = get_callback_port(login_proc); http_get('http://localhost:' + callback_port + '/callback?error=access_denied&error_description=User+denied+access'); _, stderr, exit_code = login_proc.wait(); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'access_denied' in stderr OR 'User denied access' in stderr; assert not is_port_listening(callback_port)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc login parses the error envelope when POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns an HTTP error and prints the human-readable message.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns provider list for GET /api/v1/auth/providers
A stub hub server returns HTTP 400 with {error:'invalid_code', message:'Authorization code has expired'} from POST /api/v1/auth/callback
edge_case_tests.input: Simulate successful OAuth redirect to callback server with ?code=expired-code
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'Authorization code has expired' from the error envelope
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('POST /api/v1/auth/callback').return(400, {error:'invalid_code', message:'Authorization code has expired'}); login_proc = exec_async('afc login --provider github'); callback_port = get_callback_port(login_proc); http_get('http://localhost:' + callback_port + '/callback?code=expired-code'); _, stderr, exit_code = login_proc.wait(); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'Authorization code has expired' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc login shuts down the callback server and exits non-zero cleanly when interrupted by SIGINT while waiting for the OAuth callback.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns a provider list including github
afc login process is running and waiting for callback
edge_case_tests.input: Send SIGINT to the afc login process after it starts the callback server
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with non-zero exit code; callback port is no longer listening; no orphaned child processes remain
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: login_proc = exec_async('afc login --provider github'); callback_port = get_callback_port(login_proc); sleep(0.5); login_proc.send_signal(SIGINT); _, _, exit_code = login_proc.wait(timeout=5); assert exit_code != 0; assert not is_port_listening(callback_port); assert no_orphaned_processes_on(callback_port)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc login prints the requested provider name and lists available providers in the error when the requested provider is not in the hub's provider list.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns ['github'] from GET /api/v1/auth/providers
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message indicating 'gitlab' was not found and lists 'github' as an available provider
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('GET /api/v1/auth/providers').return({providers:['github']}); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc login --provider gitlab'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'gitlab' in stderr; assert 'github' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys create prints a membership error to stderr when the user is not a member of the specified workspace.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 403 with {error:'forbidden', message:'User is not a member of workspace ws-999'} for the create key endpoint
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message about membership or access to workspace ws-999
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on_create_key().return(403, {error:'forbidden', message:'User is not a member of workspace ws-999'}); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys create --workspace ws-999 --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'member' in stderr.lower() OR 'ws-999' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys create prints a usage error and exits non-zero when the --workspace flag is missing.
edge_case_tests.input: Run `afc keys create --api-key myapikey` without the --workspace flag
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message indicating --workspace is required
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys create --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'workspace' in stderr.lower()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys create prints a missing credential error and exits non-zero when neither --api-key nor AF_HUB_API_KEY is provided.
edge_case_tests.input: Run `afc keys create --workspace ws-123` with no --api-key flag and AF_HUB_API_KEY unset
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message about missing authentication credentials
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: unset_env('AF_HUB_API_KEY'); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys create --workspace ws-123'); assert exit_code != 0; assert any(word in stderr.lower() for word in ['api-key', 'credential', 'auth', 'AF_HUB_API_KEY'])

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys list parses the error envelope and prints a human-readable message to stderr when the hub returns an HTTP error such as 401.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 401 with {error:'unauthorized', message:'Invalid API key'} from GET /api/v1/keys
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'Invalid API key' from the parsed error envelope
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('GET /api/v1/keys').return(401, {error:'unauthorized', message:'Invalid API key'}); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys list --api-key invalid-key'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'Invalid API key' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys refresh parses the hub error envelope and exits non-zero when the key_id does not exist or is not owned by the user.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 404 with {error:'not_found', message:'Key not found'} for the refresh endpoint
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'Key not found' from the parsed error envelope
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('POST /api/v1/keys/nonexistent-key/refresh').return(404, {error:'not_found', message:'Key not found'}); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys refresh nonexistent-key --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'Key not found' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys refresh prints a usage error and exits non-zero when no key_id argument is provided.
edge_case_tests.input: Run `afc keys refresh --api-key myapikey` without a key_id argument
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message indicating a key ID is required
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys refresh --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'key' in stderr.lower() OR 'argument' in stderr.lower() OR 'required' in stderr.lower()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys revoke parses the hub error envelope and exits non-zero when the key_id does not exist or is not owned by the user.
preconditions: A stub hub server returns HTTP 404 with {error:'not_found', message:'Key not found'} for the revoke endpoint
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains 'Key not found' from the parsed error envelope
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stub.on('DELETE /api/v1/keys/nonexistent-key').return(404, {error:'not_found', message:'Key not found'}); _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys revoke nonexistent-key --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'Key not found' in stderr

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that afc keys revoke prints a usage error and exits non-zero when no key_id argument is provided.
edge_case_tests.input: Run `afc keys revoke --api-key myapikey` without a key_id argument
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains a message indicating a key ID argument is required
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: _, stderr, exit_code = exec_capture('afc keys revoke --api-key myapikey'); assert exit_code != 0; assert 'key' in stderr.lower() OR 'argument' in stderr.lower() OR 'required' in stderr.lower()

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for the successful OAuth login flow: provider validation, callback server, browser open, code exchange, and user object output.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `afc login --provider github --hub-url http://stub-hub:9001` against a stub hub server that serves the full OAuth flow
real_components: local callback HTTP server inside afc
mockable: OAuth provider redirect (simulated HTTP GET to callback URL)
expected_effects: afc issues GET /api/v1/auth/providers and receives provider list containing github
afc starts a local callback server on a randomly chosen available port
afc invokes the platform browser-open command with the GitHub authorization URL containing redirect_uri pointing to the local callback port
Simulated OAuth redirect delivers ?code=test-auth-code to the local callback server
afc issues POST /api/v1/auth/callback with the authorization code to the stub hub
afc prints the returned user object as valid JSON to stdout
afc exits with exit code 0
The callback server port is no longer listening after exit

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: create an API key with workspace, label, and expiry, then list all keys for the authenticated user.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `afc keys create --workspace ws-123 --label ci-bot --expires 30 --api-key testkey --hub-url http://stub-hub:9001`, then run `afc keys list --api-key testkey --hub-url http://stub-hub:9001`
mockable: POST /api/v1/keys endpoint (stub hub returning full key object with plaintext secret)
GET /api/v1/keys endpoint (stub hub returning array of key objects)
expected_effects: afc keys create sends authenticated POST request with workspace_id=ws-123, label=ci-bot, expires_days=30
stdout of keys create contains valid JSON key object including plaintext secret field
keys create exits with exit code 0

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: refresh an existing API key to get a new secret, then revoke it and confirm via stderr message.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `afc keys refresh key-abc --api-key testkey --hub-url http://stub-hub:9001`, then `afc keys revoke key-abc --api-key testkey --hub-url http://stub-hub:9001`
mockable: Refresh endpoint POST /api/v1/keys/key-abc/refresh (stub hub returning updated key with new secret)
expected_effects: afc keys refresh sends authenticated request for key-abc
stdout of keys refresh contains valid JSON with new plaintext secret
keys refresh exits with exit code 0
afc keys revoke sends authenticated revoke request for key-abc
stderr of keys revoke contains a confirmation message referencing successful revocation
stdout of keys revoke is empty or contains no key data
keys revoke exits with exit code 0

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test for the OAuth callback timeout path: login starts, waits for callback, times out, cleans up, and exits non-zero with a retry suggestion.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run `afc login --provider github --hub-url http://stub-hub:9001` with the callback timeout set to a short duration (e.g. 5 seconds via test env var) and never send the OAuth redirect
real_components: local callback HTTP server inside afc
expected_effects: afc fetches and validates the provider list
afc starts callback server and invokes browser open command
afc waits for the callback and times out after the configured duration
stderr contains a timeout error message
stderr contains a retry suggestion
The callback server port is released after timeout
Process exits with a non-zero exit code
